title: Statistical Calculations
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program using `@stdlib/math` that computes statistics on the array [4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42]. Import and use `sqrt`, `floor`, and `pow` from @stdlib/math. Calculate:
1. Mean (sum / count)
2. Variance (mean of squared differences from mean)
3. Standard deviation (square root of variance)
4. Print each on its own line. Round std dev down to 2 decimal places using floor(x * 100) / 100. Print mean and variance as-is (no explicit rounding).

Format: "mean: VALUE", "variance: VALUE", "stddev: VALUE"
